{{日本の氏族
|家名=Minosaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko
<!-- |家紋= family crest image -->
|家紋名称=
|本姓={{ill|Mino clan|ja|三野氏}} ([[Atai (kabane)|Atai]])
|家祖={{ill|Yatsuri-no-Mikoto|ja|八瓜入日子王|lt=Yatsuri-no-Mikoto}}<br/>(son of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Miko|ja|彦坐王}})
|種別=[[Imperial-descent clan|Imperial-descent]]
|出身地=[[Yamato Province]]
|根拠地=Minosaki Province (later the central-western part of [[Mino Province]])
|人物=See [[#Clan|Clan]]
|支流=Nagahatabe-no-Muraji
}}
{{nihongo|'''Minosaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko'''|三野前国造|''mino no saki no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''mino no saki no kokuzō'', ''mino no michinokuchi no kuni no miyatsuko'', ''mino no michinokuchi kokuzō''}} was a [[kuni no miyatsuko]] who ruled [[#Territory|Minosaki Province]]. It is considered the same kuni no miyatsuko as the '''Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' and the '''{{ill|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|本巣国造}}'''.<ref>"Ancient and Medieval, Chapter 1: The Kamo Area before the Taika Era, Section 3: Kuni no Miyatsuko and Agatanushi", in ''Minokamo City History: General History'', [[Minokamo, Gifu|Minokamo City]], 1980, p. 202.</ref><ref>"Chapter 6: Before the Taika Era, Section 2: Kuni no Miyatsuko and Agatanushi", in ''Kakamigahara City History: General History — Nature, Prehistory, Ancient, and Medieval'', Kakamigahara City Board of Education, 1986, pp. 181–184.</ref><ref name="kamo">[[Hōga Toshio]], "Branching and Activities of the Minosaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko and Related Lines", in ''Kodai Shizoku no Kenkyū 18: The Kamo and Hattori Clans — Lineages Descended from Sukunabikona-no-Kami'', Aogaki Shuppan, 2021, pp. 81–82.</ref>

== Overview ==
=== Name ===
Recorded as '''Minosaki no Kuni no Miyatsuko''' in the "Kokuzō Hongi" of the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}}.

=== Ancestry ===
* The "Kokuzō Hongi" of the ''Sendai Kuji Hongi'' records that '''{{ill|Yatsuri-no-Mikoto|ja|八瓜命}}''', son of {{ill|Hikoimasu-no-Miko|ja|彦坐王}}, was appointed kuni no miyatsuko during the reign of [[Emperor Kaika]].
* In the ''[[Kojiki]]'' chapter on Emperor Kaika, {{ill|Kamu-Ōne-no-Miko|ja|神大根王|lt=Kamu-Ōne-no-Miko}} (Yatsuri-no-Iribiko-no-Miko) is recorded as the ancestor of the {{ill|Motosu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|本巣国造}} and Nagahatabe-no-Muraji; in the [[Emperor Keikō]] chapter, Ōne-no-Miko is recorded as the ancestor of the Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko; and in the Keikō chapter of the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', Kanpone is given as the Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko — so the Minosaki, Motosu, and Mino no Kuni no Miyatsuko appear to have been the same kuni no miyatsuko.

=== Clan ===
The '''{{ill|Mino clan|ja|美濃氏}}''' (''mino-uji''; [[kabane]] of [[Atai (kabane)|Atai]]); also written '''Mino clan''' (三野氏) and '''Mino clan''' (御野氏). In the 6th month of [[Jingo-keiun]] 2 ([[768]]), female officials including '''Mino-no-Atai Tamamushi''' (''shōsō'' (chief palace cleaner), [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Upper Grade) were appointed kuni no miyatsuko of their native provinces. Additionally, in the "Mino Province, Kata District, Katakata-ri Household Register", a kuni no miyatsuko Ōba appears along with a mother-and-daughter pair, Muna and Koma; Koma appears in the 4th month of [[Hōki]] 1 ([[770]]) as kuni no miyatsuko Oma, assistant magistrate ({{ill|shōryō|ja|少領}}) of {{ill|Kata District|ja|方県郡}}, of outer Junior Sixth Rank, Lower Grade.

== Base ==
The kuni no miyatsuko's base was in '''{{ill|Fuwa District|ja|不破郡}}''' of [[Mino Province]], present-day [[Tarui, Gifu|Tarui-chō]] in [[Gifu Prefecture]].

=== Territory ===
The kuni no miyatsuko's territory was the region then known as '''Minosaki Province''', corresponding to the later Fuwa District, {{ill|Yōrō District|ja|養老郡}}, and neighboring districts of Mino Province. To the north, {{ill|Ikeda District, Mino|ja|池田郡|lt=Ikeda District}} and {{ill|Ōno District, Mino|ja|大野郡 (美濃国)|lt=Ōno District}} were the domain of the {{ill|Nukata no Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|額田国造}}.

== Tutelary Shrine ==
'''[[Nangū Taisha]]''', the [[ichinomiya]] of [[Mino Province]], which enshrines the kuni no miyatsuko's ancestral deity.

== Notes ==
{{脚注ヘルプ}}
{{Reflist}}

== References ==
{{No footnotes|section=1|date=11 September 2019}}
{{ページ番号|section=1|date=11 September 2019}}
* ''Kokushi Daijiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]).
* Sakamoto Tarō and Hirano Kunio, ''Nihon Kodai Ujizoku Jinmei Jiten'' ([[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]).
* ''Nihonshi Kōjiten'' ([[Yamakawa Shuppansha]]).
* ''Shintō Daijiten'' ([[Rinsen Shoten]]).

== See also ==
* {{ill|List of Kuni no Miyatsuko|ja|国造の一覧}}

{{Japanese-history-stub}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みののさきのくにのみやつこ}}
[[Category:美濃国]]
[[Category:岐阜県の歴史]]
[[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]]
[[ja:三野前国造]]
